title: manihot esculenta subsp. peruviana
intro: Manihot esculenta subsp. peruviana, also known as cassava or yuca, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family that is native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which help to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of Manihot esculenta subsp. peruviana include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions.
